preamble: The transformation of the alveolar nasal /n/ into a glottal stop /ʔ/, a process of debuccalization where the oral articulation is lost, leaving only the glottal closure.

examples.note: Regular shift of final nasals to glottal stops in some dialects.

sources: Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
Burenhult, N. (2005). A Grammar of Kensiu.
